purpose: Database design guidance for AI-assisted software delivery across business, content, and mobile products.

universal_principles.title: Start from business workflow, not screens
universal_principles.why: Screens change faster than core business operations.
apply: List real workflow steps before tables
Convert each business event into entities
Keep page specs separate from data ownership
avoid: storing repeated product columns on orders

universal_principles.title: Split data into Core Platform, Business Modules, and Channels
universal_principles.why: Most products grow from one channel into multiple apps, portals, and modules.
apply: Keep users, roles, organizations, files, notifications, audit logs in core
Keep commerce, CMS, inventory, booking, delivery, mobile, and accounting modular
Treat website, admin, mobile, POS, and API as channels
avoid: mixing every domain in one module

universal_principles.title: Model relationships with primary keys, foreign keys, and constraints
universal_principles.why: The database must protect integrity even when imports, scripts, or APIs bypass normal UI flows.
apply: Use PKs on every table
Use FKs for important relationships
Use unique, not-null, check constraints where supported
Use pivot tables for many-to-many

universal_principles.title: Normalize master data but snapshot historical operations
universal_principles.why: Invoices, orders, payments, and published content must keep historical truth after master data changes.
apply: Normalize customers, products, categories, authors
Snapshot product name, SKU, price, tax, customer billing info inside order/invoice lines
Use revision tables for content
avoid: rendering old invoices from current product data
duplicating master data everywhere without reason

universal_principles.title: Design status, history, and workflow tables explicitly
universal_principles.why: Operational systems need traceable transitions and approvals.
apply: Define allowed status transitions
Add status history for orders, payments, articles, tickets, appointments
Use approval_requests and approval_steps for multi-step approvals
avoid: invalid transitions such as delivered to pending

universal_principles.title: Add audit logs and activity timelines early
universal_principles.why: Commercial systems need accountability, support history, and incident investigation.
apply: Use audit_logs for who changed what
Use activity_logs for customer/order/ticket journey events
Capture old_values and new_values as JSON where appropriate
avoid: adding audit only after a production dispute
mixing legal audit and operational timeline without distinction

universal_principles.title: Treat money, ledgers, wallets, loyalty, and stock as movements
universal_principles.why: Balances without transaction history cannot be audited or reconciled.
apply: Use decimal or integer cents, never float
Use wallet_transactions and loyalty_transactions
Use stock_movements plus stock_balances
Do not delete financial operations
avoid: products.stock_quantity as the only inventory truth

universal_principles.title: Plan indexes, read models, summaries, search, and reporting
universal_principles.why: Operational tables should not carry every heavy report and screen query forever.
apply: Index frequent filters and joins
Use composite indexes from real queries
Use summary tables for daily/monthly metrics
Use read models for expensive list screens
Use search engines for larger search needs
avoid: running BI reports directly on hot transactional tables

universal_principles.title: Design for security, privacy, and multi-tenancy before growth
universal_principles.why: Adding tenant separation or sensitive-data protection late is expensive and risky.
apply: Add organization_id to tenant-owned tables
Keep users distinct from customers, employees, drivers, vendors, and authors
Hash passwords
Do not store card data
Encrypt or hash tokens where appropriate
avoid: single users.type table for every actor detail
sensitive data in unprotected generic JSON

universal_principles.title: Use transactions, idempotency, outbox, and integration logs
universal_principles.why: Payments, webhooks, mobile retries, inventory, and notifications fail in real systems.
apply: Wrap sensitive workflows in transactions
Use idempotency_keys for create/payment/webhook APIs
Use outbox_events for reliable async work
Store integration_logs and external IDs

universal_principles.title: Plan migrations, backups, time zones, archiving, and restore tests
universal_principles.why: Production data needs traceable change and recovery strategy.
apply: Use migrations for all schema changes
Store timestamps in UTC
Add organization/branch/user timezone when needed
Plan backup and restore tests
Archive old logs and stats

approval_checklist: Every table has a primary key.
Tenant-owned tables include organization_id when SaaS or multi-company is possible.
Tables have created_at and updated_at where appropriate.
Soft delete is used only on suitable master data.
Money uses decimal or integer minor units, never float.
Orders, invoices, payments, tax, and published content preserve snapshots or revisions.
Inventory uses stock_movements as the source of truth.
Important operations have audit_logs and status_history.
High-volume screens have indexes, pagination, and read-model strategy.
Reports use summaries/read models when operational joins become expensive.
Sensitive data is separated, hashed, encrypted, or delegated to providers.
Payments, inventory, wallets, bookings, and external webhooks use transactions and idempotency.
Outbox events and integration logs exist for async/provider work.
Migrations, backup, restore testing, timezone, archiving, and ERD are planned before implementation.
